visa_types.name: Business/Official visit
visa_types.documents_required:
Documents Required
Step‑by‑Step Visa Document Preparation & Upload Instructions
1. Review the Checklist
Carefully review the visa category checklist. Download the checklist here – the checklist.
2. Scanning Rules (Very Important)
- File format: PDF only
- Each category = one PDF file
- Documents inside the same category must be combined into a single PDF
- Ensure documents are: 
- Clear and readable
- In color where possible
- Correctly oriented (not upside down)
- Do not include blank pages
3. Document Categories & What to Include
A. Employment Details (One PDF)
Include only what applies to you:
- Recent pay slips
- Employment letter (on company letterhead)
- Business registration documents if self employed (LLC / PE/ other)
- Pension certificate (if applicable)
PDF name example:
Employment Details.pdf
B. Family Details (One PDF)
Include:
- Birth certificate (mandatory for children under 18)
- Marriage certificate (if applicable)
- Passport copy of partner (only if partner is Cypriot)
PDF name example:
Family Details.pdf
C. Financial Documents (One PDF)
Include:
- Bank statements or bank certificate
- Tax returns
- Sponsorship letter (if applicable)
PDF name example:
Financial Documents.pdf
D. Journey / Travel Details (One PDF)
Include:
- Flight itinerary
- Hotel booking
- Travel itinerary
- Travel insurance
- Invitation letter (if applicable)
PDF name example:
Travel Details.pdf
E. Passport Copy (One PDF)
- A clear scan of all passport pages
- Cover page
- Bio page
- All stamped pages
- Blank pages
PDF name example:
Passport Copy All Pages.pdf
F. Visa Application Form (One PDF)
- Completed visa application form
- Biometric photo attached (3.5 × 4.5 cm) in the designated section
- All applicable fields fully completed
PDF name example:
Visa Application Form.pdf
G. Signed Checklist (One PDF)
- Print the checklist
- Tick only the documents you are submitting
- Sign the checklist
- Scan and upload
PDF name example:
Signed Checklist.pdf
H. Personal Details (One PDF)
Include:
- Proof of assets
- Ownership documents (property, vehicle, etc.), if applicable
PDF name example: 
Personal Details.pdf
4. Final Upload Checklist
Before uploading, confirm:
- All documents are scanned into the correct PDF category
- Only relevant documents are included
- All PDFs are readable and complete
- The checklist is signed and ticked correctly
5. Upload to VFS Portal
Upload each PDF under its respective category as listed above when submitting your visa application through the VFS Global portal.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[cyp > arm > en > Tourist > Visa Fees]
Please note:
- Visa fee for visa for Armenian citizens remains the same – 35 euro (also for citizens of countries which has visa facilitation agreements with EU, namely: Albania, Azerbaijan, Bosnia Herzegovina, FYROM, Georgia, Moldova, Montenegro, Serbia, Ukraine). 
- Visa fee for all other nationalities, for Schengen visa increases to 90 euro (45 euro for children 6 to 12 years).
VFS service charge:
Apart from the visa fee, VFS service charge of the equivalent of 5 Euro exclusive of VAT paid in AMD per application will be levied for applicants applying at the visa application centre.
The visa charge is waived for the following applicants:
- Children under 12 years of age
- Persons who travel to study
- Pensioners (pension book is required)
- Relatives of EU citizens (direct ties)
- Cultural, sport journeys (with corresponding invitation and job reference)
- Volunteers
- Disabled persons (1-st, 2-nd, 3-rd groups of disability)
- Accompanying persons of the disabled (if necessary)
For more information, please visit the link below: EUR-Lex - 22013A1031(01) - EN - EUR-Lex (europa.eu)
The service charge is waived for the following applicants:
- Children under 12 years of age
- Disabled persons (1-st and 2-nd group of disability) and persons accompanying them, if necessary
- Relatives of EU citizens (husband/wife/children under 21)
Please Note:
- In all above mentioned cases fees are waived if the required documents are available to prove the truth of state.
- Visa fee and VFS service charge must be paid at the visa application centre in cash/by credit card and are non-refundable.
- The applicable fees are charged in Armenian Drams.
THE DOCUMENTS CAN BE SUBMITTED NOT EARLIER THAN 6 MONTHS BEFORE THE INTENDED DEPARTURE DATE.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Please be informed that the Embassy of Cyprus in Armenia is accepting documents for the trips to Cyprus.
If you wish to visit Cyprus for a short period of time and if you come from a country with a visa requirement to enter Cyprus, you must obtain a visa prior to entry. Armenian nationals require a visa to enter Cyprus.
Applicant must choose correct Purpose of travel to Cyprus while applying for visa and the decision must be made by the applicants themselves – Visa Application Centre staff will not be able to assist in taking this decision. In case of any questions regarding the visa application the Embassy/ Consulate General may require an additional interview with the applicant.
Step 1: Prepare and upload documents
- Visit the official portal: https://visa.vfsglobal.com/arm/en/cyp/ (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/arm/en/cyp/)
- Navigate to the “2. Prepare your documents” section.
- Carefully review the document checklist for your visa category. 
- Complete the visa application form and attach your recent biometric photo (3.5x4.5 cm) and upload the form and all required supporting documents as outlined under: “2. Prepare your documents”
Step 2: Document review and Appointment booking 
- Once your documents have been reviewed and meet the required criteria, you will receive confirmation to schedule a biometric appointment.
- To book your appointment, you must create an account here: https://visa.vfsglobal.com/arm/en/cyp/login (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/arm/en/cyp/login)
Please note: Biometric appointments can only be booked after successful document verification.
We strongly encourage all applicants to follow the updated process carefully to avoid delays and ensure a smooth application experience.
For more details, please visit the official VFS Global website 
- https://visa.vfsglobal.com/arm/en/cyp/ (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/arm/en/cyp/)
VFS is not at liberty to consider the admissibility of an application. If necessary documents are missing the Embassy can decide not to process the application even if VFS received and forwarded it. In such cases VFS informs the applicants about the latest requirements regarding the documents given from the Embassy and it is up to applicant to decide to hand in the documents that way or not.
Please be informed that learning of your documents may take some time. For saving your time please present the package of documents in the right order. Follow the instructions presented in the website for accepting your documents faster. Children under 12 years of age may not be present during the submission of documents in VAC. They are exempted from giving the fingerprints.
The normal processing time for applications submitted in Yerevan is 10- calendar days from the day of submission. In case of incomplete documentation or in case the Embassy/ Consulate General wishes to see you in person before making a decision on your application, the processing time may increase.
A visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Cyprus for a limited period of time (90 days within a period of 180 days). If you wish to reside in Cyprus for an extended period of time (more than 90 days), you need to apply for a National Visa.
The possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right of entry to a country. The visa holder is requested to present proof that they fulfill the entry conditions at the border (valid travel document, valid visa, and valid purpose of travel with adequate means of travel).
Passport has to be valid for at least three months beyond the intended stay in Cyprus and needs to contain at least two empty pages available for visa and/or permit sticker. A new passport must be obtained before submission of your application, if the said conditions are not met.
Also please check below information about Visa code
- Voluntary use of ESP (principle of direct access) Art. 17 (5) Visa Code; VFA provisions w/a
- Competent Member State rules Art. 5 Visa Code
- List of supporting documents to be provided by the applicant Art. 43 (4)(b) Visa Code; Directive 2004/38; harmonized list, w/a
- Visa Fee and Service fee amount Art. 17(2) Visa Code
- Visa fee waivers Art. 16 Visa Code; VFA's visa fee exemptions; Directive 2004/38
- 6.Time-frame of the Process (delivery to the embassy next working day + deadline(s) for decision making max 15 days or less if VFA)Art. 43(11) Visa Code; Art. 23 Visa Code; Directive 2004/38) Art. 43(11) Visa Code; Art. 23 Visa Code; Directive 2004/38
- Optional character of additional services Art. 17(4) Visa Code
You can download full visa code here

## Documents Required
Documents Required
Step‑by‑Step Visa Document Preparation & Upload Instructions
1. Review the Checklist
Carefully review the visa category checklist. Download the checklist here – the checklist.
2. Scanning Rules (Very Important)
- File format: PDF only
- Each category = one PDF file
- Documents inside the same category must be combined into a single PDF
- Ensure documents are: 
- Clear and readable
- In color where possible
- Correctly oriented (not upside down)
- Do not include blank pages
3. Document Categories & What to Include
A. Employment Details (One PDF)
Include only what applies to you:
- Recent pay slips
- Employment letter (on company letterhead)
- Business registration documents if self employed (LLC / PE/ other)
- Pension certificate (if applicable)

F. Visa Application Form (One PDF)

4. Final Upload Checklist

- All documents are scanned into the correct PDF category
- Only relevant documents are included
- All PDFs are readable and complete
- The checklist is signed and ticked correctly
5. Upload to VFS Portal
Upload each PDF under its respective category as listed above when submitting your visa application through the VFS Global portal.

## Photo Specifications
Please read the specifications for photographs carefully before submitting your visa application. The photo needs to display a full frontal view of the face Size of the photo must be 3.5 x 4.5 and the face must be 70% of the photo. Please look directly into the camera with a neutral expression and without smiling.
The facial image must be sharp, clear, and with sufficient contrast. Please avoid photos with reflections or shadows on the face or with red eyes. The background should be neutral and light, providing sufficient contrast to the face and hair (a neutral gray/white). Reflection from glasses, sunglasses, or tinted glasses is not permitted. The eyes cannot be hidden by the glass frames. Large jewelry is not allowed.
The photo needs to be printed on high-quality paper; the colours must present a natural appearance and skin tone. The photo must not have kinks, scratches or stains on it.

## Processing Time
Processing time for the Embassy of the Republic of Cyprus in Armenia is within 10 calendar days up to 2 months. Applications shall be lodged no more than 6 months before the start of the intended visit and, as a rule, no later than 14 calendar days before the start of the intended visit. Each visa application is considered individually, so, in connection with this, a decision-making term can vary.

Application form

## SPECIFIC REQUIREMENTS DEPENDING ON THE PURPOSE OF TRAVEL
- Tourism (i) Proof of accommodation in the Republic of Cyprus
- Business (i) − Invitation by an inviting company / legal person / branch in Cyprus
- The invitation must specify whether the accommodation is provided by the inviting party. If this is not the case , a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses of the stay; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months are required
- Private entrepreneurship: a registration of the applicant's company in the state register
- Private visits (family/friends) (i) − Invitation/sponsorship form validated by the competent authorities in Cyprus with confirmation that the inviting / sponsoring party has sufficient financial means. Cyprus requires a special form 
- ID document of the person who signs the invitation letter in Cyprus must be attached
- The invitation/sponsorship form must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation/ sponsorship form must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Visiting close relatives: spouses, children (including adopted children), parents (including legal custodians), grandparents, grandchildren visiting citizens of rmenia legally residing in Cyprus, or citizens of the Union residing in the territory of Cyprus: (i) − Invitation validated by competent authorities with confirmation that the inviting party has sufficient financial means; or formal letter of obligation 
- An official document confirming the existence of relationship with proof of his / her legal residence in the country of destination
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Travelling for medical reasons (and the need for being accompanied) (i) − An official document of the medical institution confirming medical care in that institution, the necessity of being accompanied and proof of sufficient financial means to pay the medical treatment 
- The official document must specify the details of the accommodation; in case the applicant does not stay in the hospital, a hotel booking is required.
- Transit (i) − Visa or other entry permit for the country of destination
- Members of official delegations including permanent members of such delegations who, following an official invitation addressed to Armenia, shall participate in meetings, consultations, negotiations or exchange programmes, as well as in events held in Cyprus by intergovernmental organisations:
-  (i)a letter issued by a competent authority of Armenia confirming that the applicant is a member of its delegation, respectively a permanent member of its delegation, travelling to the territory of the other Party to participate in the aforementioned events, accompanied by a copy of the official invitation
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the event organizer, a hotel booking is required 
- The letter or the official invitation must specify who covers the expenses. In case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Pupils, students, post-graduate persons and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purposes of study or educational training, including in the framework of exchange programmes as well as other school-related activities:
-  (i) a written request or a certificate of enrolment from the host university, college or school or student cards or certificates of the courses to be attended;
- The enrolment certificate must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided, a hotel booking or any other proof of accommodation is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required. 
- Journalists and technical crew accompanying them in a professional capacity 
- (i) a certificate or other document issued by a professional organisation or the applicant’s employer proving that the person concerned is a qualified journalist and stating that the purpose of the journey is to carry out journalistic work or proving that he/she is a member of the technical crew accompanying the journalist in a professional capacity;
- The certificate of the employer must indicate who covers the expenses. If the employer covers the costs, the employer's bank statement for the last three months is required. In case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Participants in international sport events and persons accompanying them in a professional capacity: (i) − a written request from the host organisation, competent authorities, national sport federations or national Olympic committees of Cyprus;
- A written request must specify the details of the accommodation; if accommodation is not arranged by the event's organizer, a hotel booking is required.
- A written request must specify who covers the costs; if the costs are covered by the event's organizer or the sending organization, its bank statement for the last three months is required.
- Members of the professions participating in international exhibitions, conferences, symposia, seminars or other similar events:
- a written request from the host organisation confirming that the person concerned is participating in the event;
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required.
- Representatives of civil society organisations and persons invited by Armenian community non-profit organisations registered in Cyprus when undertaking trips for the purposes of educational training, seminars, conferences, including in the framework of exchange programmes or Pan-Armenian and community support programmes:
- a written request issued by the host organisation, a confirmation that the person is representing the civil society organisation or participating in Pan-Armenian or community support activities and the certificate on establishment of such organisation from the relevant register issued by a state authority in accordance with the national legislation;
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Persons participating in scientific, academic, cultural or artistic activities, including university and other exchange programmes 
- (i) a written request from the host organisation to participate in the activities;
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required.
- Participants of the official exchange programmes organised by twin cities and other municipal entities:
-  (i) a written request of the Head of Administration/Mayor of these cities or municipal authorities;
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required.
- Visiting military and civil burial grounds: (i)− an official document confirming the existence and preservation of the grave as well as family or other relationship between the applicant and the buried.

Overview
Please be informed that the Embassy of Cyprus in Armenia is accepting documents for the trips to Cyprus.
If you wish to visit Cyprus for a short period of time and if you come from a country with a visa requirement to enter Cyprus, you must obtain a visa prior to entry. Armenian nationals require a visa to enter Cyprus.
Applicant must choose correct Purpose of travel to Cyprus while applying for visa and the decision must be made by the applicants themselves – Visa Application Centre staff will not be able to assist in taking this decision. In case of any questions regarding the visa application the Embassy/ Consulate General may require an additional interview with the applicant.
Step 1: Prepare and upload documents
- Visit the official portal: https://visa.vfsglobal.com/arm/en/cyp/ (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/arm/en/cyp/)
- Navigate to the “2. Prepare your documents” section.
- Carefully review the document checklist for your visa category. 
- Complete the visa application form and attach your recent biometric photo (3.5x4.5 cm) and upload the form and all required supporting documents as outlined under: “2. Prepare your documents”
Step 2: Document review and Appointment booking 
- Once your documents have been reviewed and meet the required criteria, you will receive confirmation to schedule a biometric appointment.
- To book your appointment, you must create an account here: https://visa.vfsglobal.com/arm/en/cyp/login (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/arm/en/cyp/login)
Please note: Biometric appointments can only be booked after successful document verification.
We strongly encourage all applicants to follow the updated process carefully to avoid delays and ensure a smooth application experience.
For more details, please visit the official VFS Global website 
- https://visa.vfsglobal.com/arm/en/cyp/ (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/arm/en/cyp/)
VFS is not at liberty to consider the admissibility of an application. If necessary documents are missing the Embassy can decide not to process the application even if VFS received and forwarded it. In such cases VFS informs the applicants about the latest requirements regarding the documents given from the Embassy and it is up to applicant to decide to hand in the documents that way or not.
Please be informed that learning of your documents may take some time. For saving your time please present the package of documents in the right order. Follow the instructions presented in the website for accepting your documents faster. Children under 12 years of age may not be present during the submission of documents in VAC. They are exempted from giving the fingerprints.
The normal processing time for applications submitted in Yerevan is 10- calendar days from the day of submission. In case of incomplete documentation or in case the Embassy/ Consulate General wishes to see you in person before making a decision on your application, the processing time may increase.
A visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Cyprus for a limited period of time (90 days within a period of 180 days). If you wish to reside in Cyprus for an extended period of time (more than 90 days), you need to apply for a National Visa.
The possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right of entry to a country. The visa holder is requested to present proof that they fulfill the entry conditions at the border (valid travel document, valid visa, and valid purpose of travel with adequate means of travel).
Passport has to be valid for at least three months beyond the intended stay in Cyprus and needs to contain at least two empty pages available for visa and/or permit sticker. A new passport must be obtained before submission of your application, if the said conditions are not met.
Also please check below information about Visa code
- Voluntary use of ESP (principle of direct access) Art. 17 (5) Visa Code; VFA provisions w/a
- Competent Member State rules Art. 5 Visa Code
- List of supporting documents to be provided by the applicant Art. 43 (4)(b) Visa Code; Directive 2004/38; harmonized list, w/a
- Visa Fee and Service fee amount Art. 17(2) Visa Code
- Visa fee waivers Art. 16 Visa Code; VFA's visa fee exemptions; Directive 2004/38
- Time-frame of the Process (delivery to the embassy next working day  + deadline(s) for decision making 
max 15 days or less if VFA) Art. 43(11) Visa Code; Art. 23 Visa Code; Directive 2004/38
- Optional character of additional services Art. 17(4) Visa Code
You can download full visa code here

- Optional character of additional services Art. 17(4) Visa Code
You can download full visa code here

## Visa Fees

[cyp > arm > en > Tourist > Visa Fees]
Please note:
- Visa fee for visa for Armenian citizens remains the same – 35 euro (also for citizens of countries which has visa facilitation agreements with EU, namely: Albania, Azerbaijan, Bosnia Herzegovina, FYROM, Georgia, Moldova, Montenegro, Serbia, Ukraine). Visa fee for citizens of the Republic of Cape Verde is 75% from the applicable fee specified in EU legislation, i.e. €67,50.
- Visa fee for all other nationalities, for visa increases to 90 euro (45 euro for children 6 to 12 years).
VFS service charge:
Apart from the visa fee, VFS service charge of the equivalent of 5 Euros exclusive of VAT paid in AMD per application will be levied for applicants applying at the visa application centre.
The visa charge is waived for the following applicants:
- Children under 12 years of age
- Persons who travel to study
- Pensioners (pension book is required)
- Relatives of EU citizens (direct ties)
- Cultural, sport journeys (with corresponding invitation and job reference)
- Volunteers
- Persons who travel with business invitations (with corresponding invitation and job reference)
- Disabled persons (1-st, 2-nd, 3-rd groups of disability)
- Accompanying persons of the disabled (if necessary)
For more information, please visit the link below: EUR-Lex - 22013A1031(01) - EN - EUR-Lex (europa.eu)
The service charge is waived for the following applicants:
- Children under 12 years of age
- Disabled persons (1-st and 2-nd group of disability) and persons accompanying them, if necessary
- Relatives of EU citizens (husband/wife/children under 21)
Please Note:
- In all above mentioned cases fees are waived if the required documents are available to prove the truth of state.
- Visa fee and VFS service charge must be paid at the visa application centre in cash/by credit card and are non-refundable.
- The applicable fees are charged in Armenian Drams.
THE DOCUMENTS CAN BE SUBMITTED NOT EARLIER THAN 6 MONTHS BEFORE THE INTENDED DEPARTURE DATE.

## Documents Required
Step‑by‑Step Visa Document Preparation & Upload Instructions
1. Review the Checklist
Carefully review the visa category checklist. Download the checklist here – the checklist.
2. Scanning Rules (Very Important)
- File format: PDF only
- Each category = one PDF file
- Documents inside the same category must be combined into a single PDF
- Ensure documents are: 
- Clear and readable
- In color where possible
- Correctly oriented (not upside down)
- Do not include blank pages
3. Document Categories & What to Include
A. Employment Details (One PDF)
Include only what applies to you:
- Recent pay slips
- Employment letter (on company letterhead)
- Business registration documents if self employed (LLC / PE/ other)
- Pension certificate (if applicable)
PDF name example:
Employment Details.pdf
B. Family Details (One PDF)
Include:
- Birth certificate (mandatory for children under 18)
- Marriage certificate (if applicable)
- Passport copy of partner (only if partner is Cypriot)
PDF name example:
Family Details.pdf
C. Financial Documents (One PDF)
Include:
- Bank statements or bank certificate
- Tax returns
- Sponsorship letter (if applicable)
PDF name example:
Financial Documents.pdf
D. Journey / Travel Details (One PDF)
Include:
- Flight itinerary
- Hotel booking
- Travel itinerary
- Travel insurance
- Invitation letter (if applicable)
PDF name example:
Travel Details.pdf
E. Passport Copy (One PDF)
- A clear scan of all passport pages
- Cover page
- Bio page
- All stamped pages
- Blank pages
PDF name example:
Passport Copy All Pages.pdf
F. Visa Application Form (One PDF)
- Completed visa application form
- Biometric photo attached (3.5 × 4.5 cm) in the designated section
- All applicable fields fully completed
PDF name example:
Visa Application Form.pdf
G. Signed Checklist (One PDF)
- Print the checklist
- Tick only the documents you are submitting
- Sign the checklist
- Scan and upload
PDF name example:
Signed Checklist.pdf
H. Personal Details (One PDF)
Include:
- Proof of assets
- Ownership documents (property, vehicle, etc.), if applicable
PDF name example: 
Personal Details.pdf
4. Final Upload Checklist
Before uploading, confirm:
- All documents are scanned into the correct PDF category
- Only relevant documents are included
- All PDFs are readable and complete
- The checklist is signed and ticked correctly
5. Upload to VFS Portal
Upload each PDF under its respective category as listed above when submitting your visa application through the VFS Global portal.

## Photo Specifications
Please read the specifications for photographs carefully before submitting your visa application. The photo needs to display a full frontal view of the face Size of the photo must be 3.5 x 4.5 and the face must be 70% of the photo. Please look directly into the camera with a neutral expression and without smiling.
The facial image must be sharp, clear, and with sufficient contrast. Please avoid photos with reflections or shadows on the face or with red eyes. The background should be neutral and light, providing sufficient contrast to the face and hair (a neutral gray/white). Reflection from glasses, sunglasses, or tinted glasses is not permitted. The eyes cannot be hidden by the glass frames. Large jewelry is not allowed.
The photo needs to be printed on high-quality paper; the colours must present a natural appearance and skin tone. The photo must not have kinks, scratches or stains on it.

## Processing Time
Processing time for the Embassy of the Republic of Cyprus in Armenia is within 10 calendar days up to 2 months. Applications shall be lodged no more than 6 months before the start of the intended visit and, as a rule, no later than 14 calendar days before the start of the intended visit. Each visa application is considered individually, so, in connection with this, a decision-making term can vary.

## Download Forms
Assumption-of-responsibility-Form
Greek Armenian Visa Application Form 3_11_2025
Application form

## SPECIFIC REQUIREMENTS DEPENDING ON THE PURPOSE OF TRAVEL
- Tourism (i) Proof of accommodation in the Republic of Cyprus
- Business (i) − Invitation by an inviting company / legal person / branch in Cyprus
- The invitation must specify whether the accommodation is provided by the inviting party. If this is not the case , a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses of the stay; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months are required
- Private entrepreneurship: a registration of the applicant's company in the state register
- Private visits (family/friends) (i) − Invitation/sponsorship form validated by the competent authorities in Cyprus with confirmation that the inviting / sponsoring party has sufficient financial means. Cyprus requires a special form 
- ID document of the person who signs the invitation letter in Cyprus must be attached
- The invitation/sponsorship form must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation/ sponsorship form must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Visiting close relatives: spouses, children (including adopted children), parents (including legal custodians), grandparents, grandchildren visiting citizens of rmenia legally residing in Cyprus, or citizens of the Union residing in the territory of Cyprus: (i) − Invitation validated by competent authorities with confirmation that the inviting party has sufficient financial means; or formal letter of obligation 
- An official document confirming the existence of relationship with proof of his / her legal residence in the country of destination
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Travelling for medical reasons (and the need for being accompanied) (i) − An official document of the medical institution confirming medical care in that institution, the necessity of being accompanied and proof of sufficient financial means to pay the medical treatment 
- The official document must specify the details of the accommodation; in case the applicant does not stay in the hospital, a hotel booking is required.
- Transit (i) − Visa or other entry permit for the country of destination
- Members of official delegations including permanent members of such delegations who, following an official invitation addressed to Armenia, shall participate in meetings, consultations, negotiations or exchange programmes, as well as in events held in Cyprus by intergovernmental organisations:
-  (i)a letter issued by a competent authority of Armenia confirming that the applicant is a member of its delegation, respectively a permanent member of its delegation, travelling to the territory of the other Party to participate in the aforementioned events, accompanied by a copy of the official invitation
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the event organizer, a hotel booking is required 
- The letter or the official invitation must specify who covers the expenses. In case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Pupils, students, post-graduate persons and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purposes of study or educational training, including in the framework of exchange programmes as well as other school-related activities:
-  (i) a written request or a certificate of enrolment from the host university, college or school or student cards or certificates of the courses to be attended;
- The enrolment certificate must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided, a hotel booking or any other proof of accommodation is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required. 
- Journalists and technical crew accompanying them in a professional capacity 
- (i) a certificate or other document issued by a professional organisation or the applicant’s employer proving that the person concerned is a qualified journalist and stating that the purpose of the journey is to carry out journalistic work or proving that he/she is a member of the technical crew accompanying the journalist in a professional capacity;
- The certificate of the employer must indicate who covers the expenses. If the employer covers the costs, the employer's bank statement for the last three months is required. In case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Participants in international sport events and persons accompanying them in a professional capacity: (i) − a written request from the host organisation, competent authorities, national sport federations or national Olympic committees of Cyprus;
- A written request must specify the details of the accommodation; if accommodation is not arranged by the event's organizer, a hotel booking is required.
- A written request must specify who covers the costs; if the costs are covered by the event's organizer or the sending organization, its bank statement for the last three months is required.
- Members of the professions participating in international exhibitions, conferences, symposia, seminars or other similar events:
- a written request from the host organisation confirming that the person concerned is participating in the event;
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required.
- Representatives of civil society organisations and persons invited by Armenian community non-profit organisations registered in Cyprus when undertaking trips for the purposes of educational training, seminars, conferences, including in the framework of exchange programmes or Pan-Armenian and community support programmes:
- a written request issued by the host organisation, a confirmation that the person is representing the civil society organisation or participating in Pan-Armenian or community support activities and the certificate on establishment of such organisation from the relevant register issued by a state authority in accordance with the national legislation;
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Persons participating in scientific, academic, cultural or artistic activities, including university and other exchange programmes 
- (i) a written request from the host organisation to participate in the activities;
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required.
- Participants of the official exchange programmes organised by twin cities and other municipal entities:
-  (i) a written request of the Head of Administration/Mayor of these cities or municipal authorities;
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required.
- Visiting military and civil burial grounds: (i)− an official document confirming the existence and preservation of the grave as well as family or other relationship between the applicant and the buried.

[cyp > arm > en > Tourist > Visa Fees]
Please note:
- Visa fee for visa for Armenian citizens remains the same – 35 euro (also for citizens of countries which has visa facilitation agreements with EU, namely: Albania, Azerbaijan, Bosnia Herzegovina, FYROM, Georgia, Moldova, Montenegro, Serbia, Ukraine). 
- Visa fee for all other nationalities, for visa increases to 90 euro (45 euro for children 6 to 12 years).
VFS service charge:
Apart from the visa fee, VFS service charge of the equivalent of  5 Euros exclusive of VAT paid in AMD per application will be levied for applicants applying at the visa application centre.
The visa charge is waived for the following applicants:

- Relatives of EU citizens (direct ties)

## Documents Required
Documents Required
Step‑by‑Step Visa Document Preparation & Upload Instructions
1. Review the Checklist
Carefully review the visa category checklist. Download the checklist here – the checklist.
2. Scanning Rules (Very Important)
- File format: PDF only
- Each category = one PDF file
- Documents inside the same category must be combined into a single PDF
- Ensure documents are: 
- Clear and readable
- In color where possible
- Correctly oriented (not upside down)
- Do not include blank pages
3. Document Categories & What to Include
A. Employment Details (One PDF)
Include only what applies to you:
- Recent pay slips
- Employment letter (on company letterhead)
- Business registration documents if self employed (LLC / PE/ other)
- Pension certificate (if applicable)
PDF name example:
Employment Details.pdf
B. Family Details (One PDF)
Include:
- Birth certificate (mandatory for children under 18)
- Marriage certificate (if applicable)
- Passport copy of partner (only if partner is Cypriot)
PDF name example:
Family Details.pdf
C. Financial Documents (One PDF)
Include:
- Bank statements or bank certificate
- Tax returns
- Sponsorship letter (if applicable)
PDF name example:
Financial Documents.pdf
D. Journey / Travel Details (One PDF)
Include:
- Flight itinerary
- Hotel booking
- Travel itinerary
- Travel insurance
- Invitation letter (if applicable)
PDF name example:
Travel Details.pdf
E. Passport Copy (One PDF)
- A clear scan of all passport pages
- Cover page
- Bio page
- All stamped pages
- Blank pages
PDF name example:
Passport Copy All Pages.pdf
F. Visa Application Form (One PDF)
- Completed visa application form
- Biometric photo attached (3.5 × 4.5 cm) in the designated section
- All applicable fields fully completed
PDF name example:
Visa Application Form.pdf
G. Signed Checklist (One PDF)
- Print the checklist
- Tick only the documents you are submitting
- Sign the checklist
- Scan and upload
PDF name example:
Signed Checklist.pdf
H. Personal Details (One PDF)
Include:
- Proof of assets
- Ownership documents (property, vehicle, etc.), if applicable
PDF name example: 
Personal Details.pdf
4. Final Upload Checklist
Before uploading, confirm:
- All documents are scanned into the correct PDF category
- Only relevant documents are included
- All PDFs are readable and complete
- The checklist is signed and ticked correctly
5. Upload to VFS Portal
Upload each PDF under its respective category as listed above when submitting your visa application through the VFS Global portal.

## Photo Specifications
Please read the specifications for photographs carefully before submitting your visa application. The photo needs to display a full frontal view of the face Size of the photo must be 3.5 x 4.5 and the face must be 70% of the photo. Please look directly into the camera with a neutral expression and without smiling.
The facial image must be sharp, clear, and with sufficient contrast. Please avoid photos with reflections or shadows on the face or with red eyes. The background should be neutral and light, providing sufficient contrast to the face and hair (a neutral gray/white). Reflection from glasses, sunglasses, or tinted glasses is not permitted. The eyes cannot be hidden by the glass frames. Large jewelry is not allowed.
The photo needs to be printed on high-quality paper; the colours must present a natural appearance and skin tone. The photo must not have kinks, scratches or stains on it.

## Processing Time
Processing time for the Embassy of the Republic of Cyprus in Armenia is within 10 calendar days up to 2 months. Applications shall be lodged no more than 6 months before the start of the intended visit and, as a rule, no later than 14 calendar days before the start of the intended visit. Each visa application is considered individually, so, in connection with this, a decision-making term can vary.

## SPECIFIC REQUIREMENTS DEPENDING ON THE PURPOSE OF TRAVEL
- Tourism (i) Proof of accommodation in the Republic of Cyprus
- Business (i) − Invitation by an inviting company / legal person / branch in Cyprus
- The invitation must specify whether the accommodation is provided by the inviting party. If this is not the case , a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses of the stay; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months are required
- Private entrepreneurship: a registration of the applicant's company in the state register
- Private visits (family/friends) (i) − Invitation/sponsorship form validated by the competent authorities in Cyprus with confirmation that the inviting / sponsoring party has sufficient financial means. Cyprus requires a special form 
- ID document of the person who signs the invitation letter in Cyprus must be attached
- The invitation/sponsorship form must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation/ sponsorship form must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Visiting close relatives: spouses, children (including adopted children), parents (including legal custodians), grandparents, grandchildren visiting citizens of rmenia legally residing in Cyprus, or citizens of the Union residing in the territory of Cyprus: (i) − Invitation validated by competent authorities with confirmation that the inviting party has sufficient financial means; or formal letter of obligation 
- An official document confirming the existence of relationship with proof of his / her legal residence in the country of destination
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Travelling for medical reasons (and the need for being accompanied) (i) − An official document of the medical institution confirming medical care in that institution, the necessity of being accompanied and proof of sufficient financial means to pay the medical treatment 
- The official document must specify the details of the accommodation; in case the applicant does not stay in the hospital, a hotel booking is required.
- Transit (i) − Visa or other entry permit for the country of destination
- Members of official delegations including permanent members of such delegations who, following an official invitation addressed to Armenia, shall participate in meetings, consultations, negotiations or exchange programmes, as well as in events held in Cyprus by intergovernmental organisations:
-  (i)a letter issued by a competent authority of Armenia confirming that the applicant is a member of its delegation, respectively a permanent member of its delegation, travelling to the territory of the other Party to participate in the aforementioned events, accompanied by a copy of the official invitation
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the event organizer, a hotel booking is required 
- The letter or the official invitation must specify who covers the expenses. In case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Pupils, students, post-graduate persons and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purposes of study or educational training, including in the framework of exchange programmes as well as other school-related activities:
-  (i) a written request or a certificate of enrolment from the host university, college or school or student cards or certificates of the courses to be attended;
- The enrolment certificate must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided, a hotel booking or any other proof of accommodation is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required. 
- Journalists and technical crew accompanying them in a professional capacity 
- (i) a certificate or other document issued by a professional organisation or the applicant’s employer proving that the person concerned is a qualified journalist and stating that the purpose of the journey is to carry out journalistic work or proving that he/she is a member of the technical crew accompanying the journalist in a professional capacity;
- The certificate of the employer must indicate who covers the expenses. If the employer covers the costs, the employer's bank statement for the last three months is required. In case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Participants in international sport events and persons accompanying them in a professional capacity: (i) − a written request from the host organisation, competent authorities, national sport federations or national Olympic committees of Cyprus;
- A written request must specify the details of the accommodation; if accommodation is not arranged by the event's organizer, a hotel booking is required.
- A written request must specify who covers the costs; if the costs are covered by the event's organizer or the sending organization, its bank statement for the last three months is required.
- Members of the professions participating in international exhibitions, conferences, symposia, seminars or other similar events:
- a written request from the host organisation confirming that the person concerned is participating in the event;
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required.
- Representatives of civil society organisations and persons invited by Armenian community non-profit organisations registered in Cyprus when undertaking trips for the purposes of educational training, seminars, conferences, including in the framework of exchange programmes or Pan-Armenian and community support programmes:
- a written request issued by the host organisation, a confirmation that the person is representing the civil society organisation or participating in Pan-Armenian or community support activities and the certificate on establishment of such organisation from the relevant register issued by a state authority in accordance with the national legislation;
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Persons participating in scientific, academic, cultural or artistic activities, including university and other exchange programmes 
- (i) a written request from the host organisation to participate in the activities;
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required.
- Participants of the official exchange programmes organised by twin cities and other municipal entities:
-  (i) a written request of the Head of Administration/Mayor of these cities or municipal authorities;
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required.
- Visiting military and civil burial grounds: (i)− an official document confirming the existence and preservation of the grave as well as family or other relationship between the applicant and the buried.

Step‑by‑Step Visa Document Preparation & Upload Instructions
1. Review the Checklist
Carefully review the visa category checklist. Download the checklist here – the checklist.
2. Scanning Rules (Very Important)
- File format: PDF only
- Each category = one PDF file
- Documents inside the same category must be combined into a single PDF
- Ensure documents are: 
- Clear and readable
- In color where possible
- Correctly oriented (not upside down)
- Do not include blank pages
3. Document Categories & What to Include
A. Employment Details (One PDF)
Include only what applies to you:
- Recent pay slips
- Employment letter (on company letterhead)
- Business registration documents if self employed (LLC / PE/ other)
- Pension certificate (if applicable)
PDF name example:
Employment Details.pdf
B. Family Details (One PDF)
Include:
- Birth certificate (mandatory for children under 18)
- Marriage certificate (if applicable)
- Passport copy of partner (only if partner is Cypriot)
PDF name example:
Family Details.pdf
C. Financial Documents (One PDF)
Include:
- Bank statements or bank certificate
- Tax returns
- Sponsorship letter (if applicable)
PDF name example:
Financial Documents.pdf
D. Journey / Travel Details (One PDF)
Include:
- Flight itinerary
- Hotel booking
- Travel itinerary
- Travel insurance
- Invitation letter (if applicable)
PDF name example:
Travel Details.pdf
E. Passport Copy (One PDF)
- A clear scan of all passport pages
- Cover page
- Bio page
- All stamped pages
- Blank pages
PDF name example:
Passport Copy All Pages.pdf
F. Visa Application Form (One PDF)
- Completed visa application form
- Biometric photo attached (3.5 × 4.5 cm) in the designated section
- All applicable fields fully completed
PDF name example:
Visa Application Form.pdf
G. Signed Checklist (One PDF)
- Print the checklist
- Tick only the documents you are submitting
- Sign the checklist
- Scan and upload
PDF name example:
Signed Checklist.pdf
H. Personal Details (One PDF)
Include:
- Proof of assets
- Ownership documents (property, vehicle, etc.), if applicable
PDF name example: 
Personal Details.pdf
4. Final Upload Checklist
Before uploading, confirm:
- All documents are scanned into the correct PDF category
- Only relevant documents are included
- All PDFs are readable and complete
- The checklist is signed and ticked correctly
5. Upload to VFS Portal
Upload each PDF under its respective category as listed above when submitting your visa application through the VFS Global portal.

## Photo Specifications
Please read the specifications for photographs carefully before submitting your visa application. The photo needs to display a full frontal view of the face Size of the photo must be 3.5 x 4.5 and the face must be 70% of the photo. Please look directly into the camera with a neutral expression and without smiling.
The facial image must be sharp, clear, and with sufficient contrast. Please avoid photos with reflections or shadows on the face or with red eyes. The background should be neutral and light, providing sufficient contrast to the face and hair (a neutral gray/white). Reflection from glasses, sunglasses, or tinted glasses is not permitted. The eyes cannot be hidden by the glass frames. Large jewelry is not allowed.
The photo needs to be printed on high-quality paper; the colours must present a natural appearance and skin tone. The photo must not have kinks, scratches or stains on it.

## Processing Time
Processing time for the Embassy of the Republic of Cyprus in Armenia is within 10 calendar days up to 2 months. Applications shall be lodged no more than 6 months before the start of the intended visit and, as a rule, no later than 14 calendar days before the start of the intended visit. Each visa application is considered individually, so, in connection with this, a decision-making term can vary.

## SPECIFIC REQUIREMENTS DEPENDING ON THE PURPOSE OF TRAVEL
- Tourism (i) Proof of accommodation in the Republic of Cyprus
- Business (i) − Invitation by an inviting company / legal person / branch in Cyprus
- The invitation must specify whether the accommodation is provided by the inviting party. If this is not the case , a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses of the stay; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months are required
- Private entrepreneurship: a registration of the applicant's company in the state register
- Private visits (family/friends) (i) − Invitation/sponsorship form validated by the competent authorities in Cyprus with confirmation that the inviting / sponsoring party has sufficient financial means. Cyprus requires a special form 
- ID document of the person who signs the invitation letter in Cyprus must be attached
- The invitation/sponsorship form must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation/ sponsorship form must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Visiting close relatives: spouses, children (including adopted children), parents (including legal custodians), grandparents, grandchildren visiting citizens of rmenia legally residing in Cyprus, or citizens of the Union residing in the territory of Cyprus: (i) − Invitation validated by competent authorities with confirmation that the inviting party has sufficient financial means; or formal letter of obligation 
- An official document confirming the existence of relationship with proof of his / her legal residence in the country of destination
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Travelling for medical reasons (and the need for being accompanied) (i) − An official document of the medical institution confirming medical care in that institution, the necessity of being accompanied and proof of sufficient financial means to pay the medical treatment 
- The official document must specify the details of the accommodation; in case the applicant does not stay in the hospital, a hotel booking is required.
- Transit (i) − Visa or other entry permit for the country of destination
- Members of official delegations including permanent members of such delegations who, following an official invitation addressed to Armenia, shall participate in meetings, consultations, negotiations or exchange programmes, as well as in events held in Cyprus by intergovernmental organisations:
-  (i)a letter issued by a competent authority of Armenia confirming that the applicant is a member of its delegation, respectively a permanent member of its delegation, travelling to the territory of the other Party to participate in the aforementioned events, accompanied by a copy of the official invitation
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the event organizer, a hotel booking is required 
- The letter or the official invitation must specify who covers the expenses. In case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Pupils, students, post-graduate persons and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purposes of study or educational training, including in the framework of exchange programmes as well as other school-related activities:
-  (i) a written request or a certificate of enrolment from the host university, college or school or student cards or certificates of the courses to be attended;
- The enrolment certificate must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided, a hotel booking or any other proof of accommodation is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required. 
- Journalists and technical crew accompanying them in a professional capacity 
- (i) a certificate or other document issued by a professional organisation or the applicant’s employer proving that the person concerned is a qualified journalist and stating that the purpose of the journey is to carry out journalistic work or proving that he/she is a member of the technical crew accompanying the journalist in a professional capacity;
- The certificate of the employer must indicate who covers the expenses. If the employer covers the costs, the employer's bank statement for the last three months is required. In case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Participants in international sport events and persons accompanying them in a professional capacity: (i) − a written request from the host organisation, competent authorities, national sport federations or national Olympic committees of Cyprus;
- A written request must specify the details of the accommodation; if accommodation is not arranged by the event's organizer, a hotel booking is required.
- A written request must specify who covers the costs; if the costs are covered by the event's organizer or the sending organization, its bank statement for the last three months is required.
- Members of the professions participating in international exhibitions, conferences, symposia, seminars or other similar events:
- a written request from the host organisation confirming that the person concerned is participating in the event;
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required.
- Representatives of civil society organisations and persons invited by Armenian community non-profit organisations registered in Cyprus when undertaking trips for the purposes of educational training, seminars, conferences, including in the framework of exchange programmes or Pan-Armenian and community support programmes:
- a written request issued by the host organisation, a confirmation that the person is representing the civil society organisation or participating in Pan-Armenian or community support activities and the certificate on establishment of such organisation from the relevant register issued by a state authority in accordance with the national legislation;
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required
- Persons participating in scientific, academic, cultural or artistic activities, including university and other exchange programmes 
- (i) a written request from the host organisation to participate in the activities;
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required.
- Participants of the official exchange programmes organised by twin cities and other municipal entities:
-  (i) a written request of the Head of Administration/Mayor of these cities or municipal authorities;
- The invitation must specify the details of the accommodation; in case it is not provided by the inviting party, a hotel booking is required
- The invitation must specify who covers the expenses; in case the expenses are to be covered by the applicant, his/her bank statement for the last three months is required.
- Visiting military and civil burial grounds: (i)− an official document confirming the existence and preservation of the grave as well as family or other relationship between the applicant and the buried.
